publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-10-21 23:09:38 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Bibliographies, site study proposals and reports, correspondence, clippings, articles, transcripts, memos, training materials, notes, photographic contact sheets, negatives.
did.abstract: Documents Ostrander's study of architecture and planning in senior housing and its effect on the aged.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor in the Dept. of Design and Environmental Analysis, Cornell University.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, publications, reports and subject files relating to Ostrander's work in gerontology and nursing homes, particularly the New York State Veterans' Home in Oxford.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Edward Ostrander papers, #23-16-3259. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also collections #23-2-2817, #47-10-3394 and #23-16-1472 for additional biographical information.

controlaccess.subject: Barrier-free design for older people.
Nursing homes -- New York (State) -- Planning.
controlaccess.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology. Design and Environmental Analysis
New York State Veterans\' Home (Oxford, N.Y.)

did.unittitle: Series I: Gerontology Project: Six Site Study
Locations, stat summary sheets, BEH maps, transcripts, sample maps, symbol sheet
Isabella Home Proposal - correspondence, site visit notes, background booklets,
Isabella Home - Background forms, observation data research maps
Isabella Home Report: A case study - The Role of the Near Environment in Mediating Social Interaction in the Infirmary of a Geriatric Center
Working proposal - An Evaluation of the Relationship of the Physical Environment and the Aging Patient/Resident in the Long Term Care Setting
Potential research/design report - by Van Alstyne
Talk by Otto Fuchs for the seminar series An Environment for the Aging
Workshop: "Housing for the Elderly: The Relationship of Environment to the Health and Well-Being of Older Persons"
Proposals - An Evaluation of an Exiting Environmental Interface with the Aging Person in a Rural Setting
Problem comments and design solutions for all 6 sites - furniture, etc.
Student researchers training materials for 6 site study - prelim field practice
Student researchers formal training session - schedule, script forms, site list, etc.
Prelim-first visit - 02- Chenango Bridge data sheets
Director's philosophy and background data sheets - 04- El Cor Nursing Home
Prelim visit - director's philosophy - first visit - 07 - Steuben County Infirmary
Background data - first visit - 08 - Tompkins County Home
Memos on behavior mapping report, project report
Burke Rehabilitation Center - correspondence and literature
Transcriptions for the Housing Projects Series
Gerontology issues, background reading lists and memos
Speaker in the 406 Visiting Speaker Program - Robert Kastenbaum
Grant proposal from College Special Projects Fund - with Ithacare and Bethany Retirement Home in Horseheads
Series II: New Nursing Home Conference
Arpie Shelton's talk - Isabella Home NYC -Director
Selected Readings for In-Depth Week - Understanding the Aging Individual in the Physical and Social Environment
Work prior to New Nursing Home Conference and reports,
Annual reports - Dorm Authority New York State
Oxford report original copy for February 4, 1974 first ½
Oxford report original copy for February 4, 1974 2nd ½
Photos, contact prints - people and site
Typescripts from Norelco and meeting scripts
B.R. Cornell The Men at Oxford student reports
Nahemow Report: Attitude Change with Institutionalization of the Aged
Binghamton SUNY Report: Relocation Profile of Oxford Residents
Health Facilities Codes, Rules and Regulations of the State of New York
Oxford - Town and Village Comprehensive Master Plans
Oxford resident list and staff list
Oxford - relocation project forms - D. Slover
Oxford Home - report of fire safety survey
Paper - System for Design of Typical Geriatric Facility Bedroom by Robert Steinbugler
Agreement with Dormitory Authority concerning Oxford Home
Memos and correspondence re: Oxford Home
Memos and correspondence re: Oxford Home
Papers and reports by the major contributors to the Gerontology Project: Steidl, Ostrander, Koncelik and Synder,
Journal articles, reports, and other resource material from gerontology writers/researchers used as background info for the Gerontology Project
